meta.title: BRSR ↔ GRI ↔ TCFD ↔ IFRS S1/S2 Cross-Framework Mapping

frameworks.brsr: SEBI BRSR Framework (2023 format, including BRSR Core)
frameworks.ifrs: IFRS S1 (General) & IFRS S2 (Climate), effective Jan 2024

meta.id_convention: P{principle}-{section}{sequence} where section: G=Governance, E=Environment, S=Social, L=Leadership, Q=Essential

mappings.brsr_label: ESG oversight by highest governance body
mappings.gri_label: Role of the highest governance body in overseeing the management of impacts
mappings.tcfd_detail: Board oversight of climate-related risks and opportunities (Gov-a)
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S1 Para 26; IFRS S2 Para 5
mappings.notes: BRSR asks whether the highest authority has responsibility for ESG; TCFD/IFRS S2 narrow the lens to climate; GRI covers all sustainability impacts

mappings.brsr_label: Sustainability committee / designated ESG responsible body
mappings.gri_label: Governance structure; Delegation of responsibility for managing impacts
mappings.tcfd_detail: Management's role in assessing and managing climate-related risks (Gov-b)
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S1 Para 26(a); IFRS S2 Para 6(a)
mappings.notes: BRSR requires naming the committee/director; IFRS S2 expects disclosure of whether dedicated climate competence exists on the board

mappings.brsr_label: Details of fines / penalties / strictures from regulators
mappings.gri_label: Compliance with laws and regulations
mappings.tcfd_detail: Processes for identifying and assessing climate-related risks (RM-a) — regulatory risk subcategory
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for monetary value and case count; GRI 2-27 asks for instances of non-compliance; IFRS S1 captures regulation as a risk vector

mappings.brsr_label: Anti-corruption / anti-bribery policy coverage
mappings.gri_label: Operations assessed for corruption risk; Communication and training on anti-corruption
mappings.notes: Pure governance topic; BRSR asks for policy existence + percentage of workforce trained; GRI 205 is the closest international equivalent

mappings.brsr_label: Conflict of interest policy and related-party disclosures
mappings.gri_label: Conflicts of interest
mappings.tcfd_detail: Indirectly relevant to Gov-a (board independence in climate oversight)
mappings.notes: BRSR format mandates disclosure of RPT percentage; GRI asks for process to prevent/mitigate conflicts

mappings.brsr_label: Awareness programmes on ethical conduct for board, KMPs, and employees
mappings.gri_label: Embedding policy commitments; Communication and training on anti-corruption
mappings.notes: BRSR requires count of board members / KMPs / employees covered; GRI 205-2 asks for percentage by region and employee category

mappings.brsr_label: Grievance redressal mechanism for stakeholders
mappings.gri_label: Processes to remediate negative impacts; Mechanisms for seeking advice and raising concerns
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S1 Para 36 (risk identification process, broadly)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for number of complaints filed/resolved per stakeholder category; GRI expects process description plus effectiveness review

mappings.brsr_label: Transparency and disclosure practices (BRSR itself, Annual Report, Sustainability Report)
mappings.gri_label: Reporting period, frequency and contact point; Restatements; External assurance
mappings.notes: Meta-disclosure: BRSR is itself the disclosure vehicle; GRI asks for assurance status; IFRS S1 requires connected reporting with financial statements

mappings.brsr_label: R&D / capex investment in sustainable products and processes
mappings.gri_label: Direct economic value generated and distributed
mappings.tcfd_detail: Capital deployment and R&D towards climate-related opportunities (M&T-b)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for absolute ₹ value and percentage; TCFD recommends disclosing capex directed at climate-related opportunities; IFRS S2 Para 29(g) asks for internal carbon price and capital allocation

mappings.brsr_label: Sustainable sourcing — percentage of inputs sourced sustainably
mappings.gri_label: Procurement practices; Materials used; Supplier environmental assessment
mappings.tcfd_detail: Supply chain climate risks and opportunities (Strat-a)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for percentage by value; GRI 301-1 separates renewable vs non-renewable inputs; IFRS S2 covers value-chain transition risks

mappings.brsr_label: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) compliance
mappings.gri_label: Reclaimed products; Management of significant waste-related impacts
mappings.notes: India-specific compliance; EPR under Plastic Waste Management Rules and E-Waste Rules; no direct TCFD/IFRS equivalent but falls under transition risk (policy/regulation)

mappings.brsr_label: Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) for products
mappings.gri_label: Recycled input materials used; Reductions in energy requirements of products and services
mappings.tcfd_detail: Climate-related opportunities in products and services (Strat-a, Strat-b)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks whether LCA has been conducted for top products; GRI does not mandate LCA per se but captures material circularity metrics; ISSB expects product-level transition risk analysis

mappings.brsr_label: Reclaimed products and waste as percentage of products sold
mappings.gri_label: Reclaimed products and their packaging materials
mappings.tcfd_detail: Circularity metrics where material to climate strategy
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for percentage by product category; GRI 301-3 asks for reclaimed volume; TCFD only if tied to climate target

mappings.brsr_label: Total employees and workers (permanent + non-permanent, by gender)
mappings.gri_label: Employees; Workers who are not employees
mappings.tcfd_detail: Not directly addressed (unless in Just Transition context)
mappings.notes: BRSR mandates male/female/other breakdowns; GRI 2-7 additionally asks for region and contract type; TCFD/IFRS do not require workforce headcounts unless transition-related

mappings.brsr_label: Percentage of women in total workforce and in management
mappings.gri_label: Diversity of governance bodies and employees
mappings.notes: BRSR asks board and workforce level percentages; GRI 405-1 expects breakdown by category, age group, and minority group; TCFD/IFRS are climate-focused, no direct gender metric

mappings.brsr_label: Differently abled employees and workers
mappings.gri_label: Diversity of governance bodies and employees
mappings.notes: BRSR requires absolute number by permanent/non-permanent and gender; GRI includes disability within broader diversity indicators

mappings.brsr_label: Employee turnover rate (permanent employees, by gender)
mappings.gri_label: New employee hires and employee turnover
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for turnover rate by male/female; GRI 401-1 asks for new hires AND turnover by age group, gender, and region

mappings.brsr_label: Median remuneration and male-to-female remuneration ratio
mappings.gri_label: Ratio of basic salary and remuneration of women to men
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for gross median by gender for Board, KMP, and employees; GRI 405-2 asks for ratio by significant locations and employee category

mappings.brsr_label: Return-to-work and retention rate after parental leave
mappings.gri_label: Parental leave
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for male and female separately, permanent employees only; GRI 401-3 additionally asks for number entitled, number who took leave, and 12-month retention rate

mappings.brsr_label: Health and Safety Management System (OHSMS) coverage
mappings.gri_label: OHS management system; Workers covered by an OHS management system
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Para 21 (climate resilience — workforce physical risk, indirect)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks whether system is certified (ISO 45001 etc.) and percentage of workforce covered; GRI 403-1 expects system description; IFRS S2 only relevant if climate-linked occupational hazards exist

mappings.brsr_label: Recordable work-related injuries (LTIFR, fatalities)
mappings.gri_label: Work-related injuries
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for LTIFR, total recordable injuries, and fatalities separately for employees and workers; GRI 403-9 aligns closely, requiring rate and number by worker type

mappings.brsr_label: Work-related ill health cases
mappings.gri_label: Work-related ill health
mappings.tcfd_detail: Physical risk — heat stress, occupational disease (indirect climate link)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for number of fatalities and cases due to ill health for employees and workers; GRI 403-10 expects types of ill health plus hazard identification; climate link exists for heat-exposed sectors

mappings.brsr_label: Training hours per employee (by gender and category)
mappings.gri_label: Average hours of training per year per employee
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for total hours and average per employee by male/female; GRI 404-1 adds breakdown by employee category (management, non-management, etc.)

mappings.brsr_label: Employee benefits (PF, gratuity, ESI, maternity, paternity, day care)
mappings.gri_label: Benefits provided to full-time employees that are not provided to temporary or part-time employees
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for specific Indian statutory benefits plus percentage coverage; GRI 401-2 is broader but less prescriptive on benefit types

mappings.brsr_label: Membership of employees in recognized trade unions / worker associations
mappings.gri_label: Freedom of association and collective bargaining; Minimum notice periods regarding operational changes
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Para 14(a) (transition planning — workforce impact, indirect)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for percentage of permanent employees who are members; GRI 407-1 focuses on operations where rights may be at risk

mappings.brsr_label: Complaints on working conditions and safety (filed vs resolved)
mappings.gri_label: Worker participation in OHS; Processes to remediate negative impacts
mappings.notes: BRSR requires current FY and previous FY complaint counts, pending resolution; GRI 403-4 addresses worker consultation processes rather than complaint counts

mappings.brsr_label: Wages paid — equal to or above minimum wage (employees and workers)
mappings.gri_label: Ratios of standard entry level wage by gender compared to local minimum wage
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for Yes/No per category (permanent, non-permanent, male, female); GRI 202-1 asks for ratio of entry-level to minimum wage by gender and significant locations

mappings.brsr_label: Key stakeholder groups identified by the entity
mappings.gri_label: Approach to stakeholder engagement
mappings.tcfd_detail: Gov-a/Gov-b — stakeholder input into climate governance (indirect)
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S1 Para 26-27 (governance process includes stakeholder consideration)
mappings.notes: BRSR mandates listing vulnerable/marginalized stakeholders; GRI 2-29 asks for categories of stakeholders and how they are identified; TCFD/IFRS expect stakeholder consideration in governance process

mappings.brsr_label: Channels and frequency of communication with stakeholders
mappings.gri_label: Approach to stakeholder engagement
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for methods, frequency, and purpose; GRI 2-29 asks for purpose and how the engagement informs decision-making

mappings.brsr_label: Material ESG issues identified through stakeholder engagement
mappings.gri_label: Process to determine material topics; List of material topics
mappings.tcfd_detail: Strat-a — identification of climate-related risks and opportunities
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for list of material topics; GRI 3 requires a double materiality process; IFRS S1 applies financial materiality lens; BRSR materiality assessment is becoming de facto double materiality

mappings.brsr_label: Human rights training coverage (employees and value chain)
mappings.gri_label: Employee training on human rights policies or procedures
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for percentage of employees and BoD trained; GRI 412-2 asks for total hours and percentage

mappings.brsr_label: Minimum wages paid to all employees and workers
mappings.gri_label: Ratios of standard entry level wage compared to local minimum wage
mappings.notes: BRSR frames minimum wages as a human rights issue; GRI 202-1 frames it as a market presence topic; overlaps with P3-L14

mappings.brsr_label: Complaints on child labor, forced labor, involuntary labor, sexual harassment, discrimination, wages
mappings.gri_label: Incidents of discrimination; Child labor; Forced or compulsory labor
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for complaints filed/pending/resolved in current and previous FY; GRI asks for number of incidents and remediation actions

mappings.brsr_label: Human rights due diligence in operations and value chain
mappings.gri_label: Operations subject to human rights reviews; New suppliers screened; Negative social impacts in supply chain
mappings.tcfd_detail: Not directly addressed (supply chain risk assessment, indirect)
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S1 Para 36 (risk management process, broadly)
mappings.notes: BRSR Leadership asks whether the entity has a HRDD process; GRI 412-1 asks for percentage of operations assessed; GRI 414 covers supplier assessment

mappings.brsr_label: Mechanism to address human rights grievances (internal and external)
mappings.gri_label: Processes to remediate negative impacts; Mechanisms for seeking advice and raising concerns
mappings.notes: BRSR asks whether the mechanism exists for value chain partners too; GRI expects process descriptions and access to remedy

mappings.brsr_label: Total electricity consumption (MWh)
mappings.gri_label: Energy consumption within the organization
mappings.notes: GRI requires fuel-type breakdown; BRSR asks for aggregate total; IFRS S2 asks for cross-industry energy consumption metric

mappings.brsr_label: Total fuel consumption (from coal, oil, natural gas, other)
mappings.gri_label: Energy consumption within the organization
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for total fuel in petajoules; GRI 302-1 requires breakdown by non-renewable and renewable fuel types

mappings.brsr_label: Energy from other sources (biomass, waste heat, etc.)
mappings.gri_label: Energy consumption within the organization
mappings.notes: BRSR captures this as a residual category; GRI classifies renewable sources within 302-1

mappings.brsr_label: Total energy consumption and energy intensity per rupee of turnover
mappings.gri_label: Energy consumption within the organization; Energy intensity
mappings.notes: BRSR denominates intensity per ₹ turnover; GRI 302-3 allows denominator choice (revenue, units, FTE); IFRS S2 cross-industry metric requires energy consumption disclosure

mappings.brsr_label: Renewable energy as percentage of total energy consumed
mappings.gri_label: Energy consumption within the organization
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Para 29(a); IFRS S2 Appendix B Industry Metrics (sector-specific)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for percentage; GRI embeds renewable share in 302-1; IFRS S2 industry-specific metrics may require renewable percentage for energy-intensive sectors

mappings.brsr_label: PAT (Perform, Achieve, Trade) scheme compliance
mappings.gri_label: Reduction of energy consumption
mappings.tcfd_detail: Targets used to manage climate-related risks (M&T-c)
mappings.notes: India-specific BEE scheme; BRSR asks whether entity is a designated consumer under PAT and target vs achievement; closest international analog is science-based energy reduction targets

mappings.brsr_label: Total water withdrawal by source
mappings.gri_label: Water withdrawal
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Appendix B (industry-specific water metrics)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for surface, groundwater, third-party, and seawater; GRI 303-3 matches well, adding water-stress area context; IFRS S2 industry metrics require water for high-water-impact sectors

mappings.brsr_label: Water consumption and water intensity per rupee of turnover
mappings.gri_label: Water consumption
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for total consumption and intensity; GRI 303-5 reports consumption in megaliters; IFRS S2 water intensity is sector-dependent

mappings.brsr_label: Water discharge by destination and treatment method
mappings.gri_label: Water discharge
mappings.tcfd_detail: Not directly addressed (unless material to physical risk)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for discharge to surface water, groundwater, seawater, third-party, and treatment levels; GRI 303-4 matches well; IFRS only if sector-specific standard requires it

mappings.brsr_label: Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) status
mappings.gri_label: Management of water discharge-related impacts
mappings.tcfd_detail: Physical risk mitigation — water scarcity response (RM-b)
mappings.notes: India-specific regulatory requirement for certain sectors; GRI 303-2 asks for water discharge standards; TCFD/IFRS S2 relevant under physical risk management for water-scarce operations

mappings.brsr_label: Total Scope 1 GHG emissions (tCO2e)
mappings.gri_label: Direct (Scope 1) GHG emissions
mappings.notes: Highest-alignment field across all four frameworks; BRSR Core mandates Scope 1; GRI asks for gases, base year, methodology; IFRS S2 requires GHG Protocol or ISO 14064

mappings.brsr_label: Total Scope 2 GHG emissions (tCO2e)
mappings.gri_label: Energy indirect (Scope 2) GHG emissions
mappings.notes: BRSR Core mandates Scope 2; GRI 305-2 asks for location-based and market-based; IFRS S2 requires location-based and encourages market-based

mappings.brsr_label: Total Scope 3 GHG emissions (tCO2e)
mappings.gri_label: Other indirect (Scope 3) GHG emissions
mappings.tcfd_detail: Disclose Scope 3 GHG emissions where appropriate (M&T-a)
mappings.notes: BRSR Core phases in Scope 3 from FY25 for top-150 by market cap; GRI 305-3 asks for categories per GHG Protocol; IFRS S2 includes transitional relief for Scope 3 measurement

mappings.brsr_label: GHG emission intensity per rupee of turnover
mappings.gri_label: GHG emissions intensity
mappings.notes: BRSR uses ₹ turnover as denominator; GRI 305-4 allows flexibility in denominator; IFRS S2 requires intensity per unit of physical/economic output

mappings.brsr_label: GHG reduction targets and progress
mappings.gri_label: Reduction of GHG emissions
mappings.tcfd_detail: Targets used to manage climate-related risks and opportunities (M&T-c)
mappings.notes: BRSR Leadership asks whether the entity has set reduction targets; GRI 305-5 asks for absolute reductions achieved; IFRS S2 Para 33-36 expects targets, base year, interim milestones, and whether science-based

mappings.brsr_label: Carbon offsets purchased or retired
mappings.gri_label: Reduction of GHG emissions (offsets noted separately)
mappings.tcfd_detail: M&T-c — if offsets are part of net-zero strategy
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for amount of offsets and type; GRI expects offsets disclosed separately from reduction; IFRS S2 Para 36(e) explicitly requires disclosure of offset use in targets, including type and crediting scheme

mappings.brsr_label: Total waste generated (hazardous and non-hazardous, in MT)
mappings.gri_label: Waste generated
mappings.tcfd_detail: Relevant if waste-to-methane or circular economy is part of climate strategy
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for plastic, e-waste, bio-medical, construction, battery, radioactive, and other categories; GRI 306-3 asks for total by composition

mappings.brsr_label: Waste diverted from disposal (reused, recycled, other recovery, in MT)
mappings.gri_label: Waste diverted from disposal
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for recovery method breakdown; GRI 306-4 expects onsite vs offsite and method; IFRS S2 only if sector standard requires waste metrics

mappings.brsr_label: Waste directed to disposal (incineration, landfill, other, in MT)
mappings.gri_label: Waste directed to disposal
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for breakdown by disposal method; GRI 306-5 asks for incineration (with/without energy recovery), landfilling, and other

mappings.brsr_label: Air pollutant emissions (NOx, SOx, PM, others)
mappings.gri_label: Nitrogen oxides (NOx), sulfur oxides (SOx), and other significant air emissions
mappings.tcfd_detail: Not directly addressed (TCFD focuses on GHGs, not criteria pollutants)
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Appendix B (sector-specific, e.g., metals and mining)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for NOx, SOx, PM, POP, VOC, HAP, and others in MT; GRI 305-7 aligns closely; TCFD excludes non-GHG air emissions; IFRS S2 captures these only via industry-specific metrics

mappings.brsr_label: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) for projects
mappings.gri_label: Operations in areas of high biodiversity value; Operations with local community engagement and impact assessments
mappings.tcfd_detail: RM-a — identifying physical and transition risks
mappings.notes: BRSR asks whether EIA has been undertaken for expansion/new projects and if results are publicly available; GRI captures this indirectly via biodiversity and community impact; TCFD/IFRS frame it as part of risk identification

mappings.brsr_label: Biodiversity — operations in or adjacent to ecologically sensitive areas
mappings.gri_label: Operational sites in areas of high biodiversity value; Significant impacts on biodiversity
mappings.tcfd_detail: Nature-related physical risks (Strat-a, increasingly cross-referenced with TNFD)
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Para 12 (physical risks including ecosystem degradation)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for site count and whether biodiversity management plans exist; GRI 304-1/304-2 expect detailed impact descriptions; ISSB is expected to integrate TNFD-aligned biodiversity disclosures

mappings.brsr_label: Single-use plastic reduction initiatives
mappings.gri_label: Waste generation and significant waste-related impacts; Management of significant waste-related impacts
mappings.notes: India-specific regulatory push under PWM Rules 2016 (amended 2022); BRSR asks for steps taken and percentage reduction; GRI captures via waste management approach

mappings.brsr_label: Details of environmental non-compliances and penalties
mappings.gri_label: Compliance with laws and regulations
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Para 10(a) (transition risks — policy and legal)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for amount of fines and number of cases; GRI 2-27 asks for instances of non-compliance; IFRS S2 includes policy/legal transition risk

mappings.brsr_label: Climate-related financial risks and opportunities identified
mappings.gri_label: Financial implications and other risks and opportunities due to climate change
mappings.tcfd_detail: Strat-a — Climate-related risks and opportunities identified over short, medium, and long term
mappings.notes: Strongest TCFD/IFRS alignment; BRSR Core is converging toward TCFD-style climate risk disclosure; GRI 201-2 captures financial implications; IFRS S2 requires scenario context

mappings.brsr_label: Scenario analysis for climate resilience
mappings.gri_label: Financial implications due to climate change
mappings.tcfd_detail: Strat-c — Resilience of strategy under different climate scenarios, including 2°C or lower
mappings.notes: BRSR Core phased requirement; TCFD/IFRS S2 expect qualitative and, where possible, quantitative scenario analysis; GRI 201-2 asks for risks/opportunities but does not mandate scenario methodology

mappings.brsr_label: Internal carbon pricing mechanism
mappings.gri_label: No direct GRI equivalent
mappings.notes: BRSR asks whether entity uses an internal carbon price and its value; TCFD supplemental guidance recommends disclosure; IFRS S2 Para 29(g) mandates disclosure if used in decision-making; GRI has no dedicated standard

mappings.brsr_label: Trade and industry association memberships
mappings.gri_label: Membership associations
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for list of associations with basis of membership; GRI 2-28 asks the same with emphasis on significant memberships

mappings.brsr_label: Anti-competitive conduct — cases and penalties
mappings.gri_label: Legal actions for anti-competitive behavior, anti-trust, and monopoly practices
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for number of pending or completed legal proceedings; GRI 206-1 aligns closely, asking for legal actions, status, and outcomes

mappings.brsr_label: Public policy positions on ESG (advocacy, lobbying disclosures)
mappings.gri_label: Political contributions
mappings.tcfd_detail: Gov-a — if lobbying positions are relevant to climate policy advocacy
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Para 14(a)(iv) (climate-related public policy engagement as part of transition plan)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks whether the entity has taken public positions on ESG issues; GRI 415-1 focuses on political contributions; IFRS S2 captures climate-related policy engagement within transition plan disclosure

mappings.brsr_label: CSR expenditure as percentage of average net profit
mappings.gri_label: Direct economic value generated and distributed; Infrastructure investments and services supported
mappings.notes: India-specific (Companies Act Section 135); BRSR asks for 2% CSR compliance; GRI captures community investment under 201-1 and 203-1; no TCFD/IFRS equivalent

mappings.brsr_label: Input material sourced from MSMEs / small producers
mappings.gri_label: Proportion of spending on local suppliers
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for percentage of inputs sourced from small/marginalized producers; GRI 204-1 focuses on locally sourced spending; BRSR is more inclusive-growth oriented

mappings.brsr_label: Preferential procurement from disadvantaged / vulnerable groups
mappings.gri_label: Proportion of spending on local suppliers; New suppliers screened using social criteria
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for procurement policy and percentage spent; GRI does not have a direct 'disadvantaged procurement' metric; closest is local supplier spending

mappings.brsr_label: CSR projects benefiting marginalized communities (SC/ST, differently abled, women)
mappings.gri_label: Operations with local community engagement, impact assessments, and development programs
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S2 Para 14(a) (transition plan — social considerations, indirect)
mappings.notes: BRSR requires specifying beneficiary demographics; GRI 413-1 focuses on community engagement programs but without India-specific social categories; IFRS S2 Just Transition lens is emerging

mappings.brsr_label: Job creation in smaller towns (beyond Tier 1/2 cities)
mappings.gri_label: Proportion of senior management hired from the local community; Significant indirect economic impacts
mappings.notes: India-specific inclusive growth lens; BRSR asks for percentage of workforce employed in underserved geographies; GRI 202-2 is closest but focuses on senior management

mappings.brsr_label: Consumer complaints received and resolved (product/service quality, data privacy, advertising, delivery, etc.)
mappings.gri_label: Complaints regarding customer privacy; Incidents of non-compliance concerning health and safety; Non-compliance regarding marketing communications
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for complaints filed, pending, and resolved across five categories; GRI splits these across separate topic standards; no TCFD/IFRS equivalent

mappings.brsr_label: Product recall instances (voluntary and forced)
mappings.gri_label: Incidents of non-compliance concerning the health and safety impacts of products and services
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for number of recalls and reasons; GRI 416-2 asks for non-compliance incidents resulting in warnings, fines, or voluntary codes

mappings.brsr_label: Data privacy — complaints and breaches
mappings.gri_label: Substantiated complaints concerning breaches of customer privacy and losses of customer data
mappings.tcfd_detail: Not directly addressed (cyber risk is discussed in TCFD guidance for financial sector)
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S1 Para 28 (general risk/opportunity, if material)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for number of complaints from customers, regulators, and law enforcement; GRI 418-1 asks for substantiated complaints and identified data leaks/thefts

mappings.brsr_label: Cybersecurity policy and practices
mappings.gri_label: Customer privacy (tangential)
mappings.ifrs_reference: IFRS S1 Para 28 (sustainability-related risk if material to enterprise value)
mappings.notes: BRSR asks whether the entity has a cyber security policy and data breach history; no direct GRI/TCFD equivalent; IFRS S1 general requirements may capture if material

mappings.brsr_label: Product labeling and responsible advertising practices
mappings.gri_label: Requirements for product and service information and labeling; Non-compliance re: labeling; Non-compliance re: marketing
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for percentage of product categories covered by labeling practices including environmental/social info and safe use; GRI 417-1 aligns closely

mappings.brsr_label: Consumer survey / feedback mechanism outcomes
mappings.gri_label: No direct GRI equivalent
mappings.notes: BRSR Leadership asks whether entity has conducted surveys on consumer satisfaction and ESG awareness; no international framework mandates consumer satisfaction surveys

mappings.brsr_label: Safe and informed usage (beyond labeling — voluntary product stewardship)
mappings.gri_label: Assessment of health and safety impacts of product and service categories
mappings.notes: BRSR asks for percentage of product/service categories assessed for health and safety and for which disclosure is provided; GRI 416-1 directly matches
